children.label: A
children.text: assess each prisoner’s skill level (including academic, vocational, health, cognitive, interpersonal, daily living, and related reentry skills) at the beginning of the term of imprisonment of that prisoner to identify any areas in need of improvement prior to reentry;

children.label: B
children.text: generate a skills development plan for each prisoner to monitor skills enhancement and reentry readiness throughout incarceration;

children.label: C
children.text: determine program assignments for prisoners based on the areas of need identified through the assessment described in subparagraph (A);

children.label: D
children.text: ensure that priority is given to the reentry needs of high-risk populations, such as sex offenders, career criminals, and prisoners with mental health problems;

children.label: E
children.text: coordinate and collaborate with other Federal agencies and with State, Tribal, and local criminal justice agencies, community-based organizations, and faith-based organizations to help effectuate a seamless reintegration of prisoners into communities;

children.label: F
children.text: collect information about a prisoner’s family relationships, parental responsibilities, and contacts with children to help prisoners maintain important familial relationships and support systems during incarceration and after release from custody; and

children.label: G
children.text: provide incentives for prisoner participation in skills development programs.

children.label: A
children.text: the maximum allowable period in a community confinement facility; and

children.label: B
children.text: such other incentives as the Director considers appropriate (not including a reduction of the term of imprisonment).

children.heading: Identification and release assistance for Federal prisoners
children.label: b Identification and release assistance for Federal prisoners

children.heading: Obtaining identification
children.label: 1 Obtaining identification
children.text: The Director shall assist prisoners in obtaining identification prior to release from a term of imprisonment in a Federal prison or if the individual was not sentenced to a term of imprisonment in a Federal prison, prior to release from a sentence to a term in community confinement, including a social security card, driver’s license or other official photo identification, and a birth certificate.

children.heading: Assistance developing release plan
children.label: 2 Assistance developing release plan
children.text: At the request of a direct-release prisoner, a representative of the United States Probation System shall, prior to the release of that prisoner, help that prisoner develop a release plan.

children.heading: Direct-release prisoner defined
children.label: 3 Direct-release prisoner defined
children.text: In this section, the term “direct-release prisoner” means a prisoner who is scheduled for release and will not be placed in prerelease custody.

children.heading: Definition
children.label: 4 Definition
children.text: In this subsection, the term “community confinement” means residence in a community treatment center, halfway house, restitution center, mental health facility, alcohol or drug rehabilitation center, or other community facility.

children.heading: Improved reentry procedures for Federal prisoners
children.label: c Improved reentry procedures for Federal prisoners

children.text: to enhance case planning and implementation of reentry programs, policies, and guidelines;
to improve such transition to the community, including placement of such individuals in community corrections facilities; and

to foster the development of collaborative partnerships with stakeholders at the national, State, and local levels to facilitate the exchange of information and the development of resources to enhance opportunities for successful offender reentry.

children.heading: Duties of the Bureau of Prisons
children.label: d Duties of the Bureau of Prisons

children.heading: Measuring the removal of obstacles to reentry
children.label: 2 Measuring the removal of obstacles to reentry

children.heading: Coding required
children.label: A Coding required
children.text: The Director shall ensure that each institution within the Bureau of Prisons codes the reentry needs and deficits of prisoners, as identified by an assessment tool that is used to produce an individualized skills development plan for each inmate.

children.heading: Tracking
children.label: B Tracking
children.text: In carrying out this paragraph, the Director shall quantitatively track the progress in responding to the reentry needs and deficits of individual inmates.

children.heading: Annual report
children.label: C Annual report
children.text: On an annual basis, the Director shall prepare and submit to the Committee on the Judiciary of the Senate and the Committee on the Judiciary of the House of Representatives a report that documents the progress of the Bureau of Prisons in responding to the reentry needs and deficits of inmates.

children.label: i
children.text: the performance of each institution within the Bureau of Prisons in enhancing skills and resources to assist in reentry is measured and evaluated using recognized measurements; and

children.label: ii
children.text: plans for corrective action are developed and implemented as necessary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: At the end of each fiscal year, the Director shall submit to the Committee on the Judiciary of the Senate and the Committee on the Judiciary of the House of Representatives a report containing statistics demonstrating the relative reduction in recidivism for inmates released by the Bureau of Prisons within that fiscal year and the 2 prior fiscal years, comparing inmates who participated in major inmate programs (including residential drug treatment, vocational training, and prison industries) with inmates who did not participate in such programs. Such statistics shall be compiled separately for each such fiscal year.

children.heading: Scope
children.label: ii Scope
children.text: A report under this paragraph is not required to include statistics for a fiscal year that begins before .April 9, 2008

children.heading: Measure used
children.label: B Measure used
children.text: In preparing the reports required by subparagraph (A), the Director shall, in consultation with the Director of the Bureau of Justice Statistics, select a measure for recidivism (such as rearrest, reincarceration, or any other valid, evidence-based measure) that the Director considers appropriate and that is consistent with the research undertaken by the Bureau of Justice Statistics under .section 60551(b)(6) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: After the Director submits the first report required by subparagraph (A), the Director shall establish goals for reductions in recidivism rates and shall work to attain those goals.

children.label: I
children.text: a 5-year goal to increase, at a minimum, the baseline relative reduction rate of recidivism by 2 percent; and

children.label: II
children.text: a 10-year goal to increase, at a minimum, the baseline relative reduction rate of recidivism by 5 percent within 10 fiscal years.

children.heading: Format
children.label: 4 Format
children.text: Any written information that the Bureau of Prisons provides to inmates for reentry planning purposes shall use common terminology and language.

children.heading: Medical care
children.label: 5 Medical care
children.text: The Bureau of Prisons shall provide the United States Probation and Pretrial Services System with relevant information on the medical care needs and the mental health treatment needs of inmates scheduled for release from custody. The United States Probation and Pretrial Services System shall take this information into account when developing supervision plans in an effort to address the medical care and mental health care needs of such individuals. The Bureau of Prisons shall provide inmates with a sufficient amount of all necessary medications (which will normally consist of, at a minimum, a 2-week supply of such medications) upon release from custody.

children.heading: Encouragement of employment of former prisoners
children.label: e Encouragement of employment of former prisoners
children.text: The Attorney General, in consultation with the Secretary of Labor, shall take such steps as are necessary to educate employers and the one-stop partners and one-stop operators (as such terms are defined in ) that provide services at any center operated under a one-stop delivery system established under regarding incentives (including the Federal bonding program of the Department of Labor and tax credits) for hiring former Federal, State, or local prisoners.section 3102 of title 29section 3151(e) of title 29

children.heading: Elderly and family reunification for certain nonviolent offenders pilot program
children.label: g Elderly and family reunification for certain nonviolent offenders pilot program

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Attorney General shall conduct a pilot program to determine the effectiveness of removing eligible elderly offenders and eligible terminally ill offenders from Bureau of Prisons facilities and placing such offenders on home detention until the expiration of the prison term to which the offender was sentenced.

children.heading: Placement in home detention
children.label: B Placement in home detention
children.text: In carrying out a pilot program as described in subparagraph (A), the Attorney General may release some or all eligible elderly offenders and eligible terminally ill offenders from Bureau of Prisons facilities to home detention, upon written request from either the Bureau of Prisons or an eligible elderly offender or eligible terminally ill offender.

children.heading: Waiver
children.label: C Waiver
children.text: The Attorney General is authorized to waive the requirements of as necessary to provide for the release of some or all eligible elderly offenders and eligible terminally ill offenders from Bureau of Prisons facilities to home detention for the purposes of the pilot program under this subsection.section 3624 of title 18

children.heading: Violation of terms of home detention
children.label: 2 Violation of terms of home detention
children.text: A violation by an eligible elderly offender or eligible terminally ill offender of the terms of home detention (including the commission of another Federal, State, or local crime) shall result in the removal of that offender from home detention and the return of that offender to the designated Bureau of Prisons institution in which that offender was imprisoned immediately before placement on home detention under paragraph (1), or to another appropriate Bureau of Prisons institution, as determined by the Bureau of Prisons.

children.heading: Scope of pilot program
children.label: 3 Scope of pilot program
children.text: A pilot program under paragraph (1) shall be conducted through Bureau of Prisons facilities designated by the Attorney General as appropriate for the pilot program and shall be carried out during fiscal years 2019 through 2023.

children.heading: Implementation and evaluation
children.label: 4 Implementation and evaluation
children.text: The Attorney General shall monitor and evaluate each eligible elderly offender or eligible terminally ill offender placed on home detention under this section, and shall report to Congress concerning the experience with the program at the end of the period described in paragraph (3). The Administrative Office of the United States Courts and the United States probation offices shall provide such assistance and carry out such functions as the Attorney General may request in monitoring, supervising, providing services to, and evaluating eligible elderly offenders and eligible terminally ill offenders released to home detention under this section.

children.label: i
children.text: who is not less than 60 years of age;

children.label: ii
children.text: who is serving a term of imprisonment that is not life imprisonment based on conviction for an offense or offenses that do not include any crime of violence (as defined in ), sex offense (as defined in ), offense described in , or offense under chapter 37 of title 18, and has served ⅔ of the term of imprisonment to which the offender was sentenced;section 16 of title 18section 20911(5) of this titlesection 2332b(g)(5)(B) of title 18

children.label: iii
children.text: who has not been convicted in the past of any Federal or State crime of violence, sex offense, or other offense described in clause (ii);

children.label: iv
children.text: who has not been determined by the Bureau of Prisons, on the basis of information the Bureau uses to make custody classifications, and in the sole discretion of the Bureau, to have a history of violence, or of engaging in conduct constituting a sex offense or other offense described in clause (ii);

children.label: v
children.text: who has not escaped, or attempted to escape, from a Bureau of Prisons institution;

children.label: vi
children.text: with respect to whom the Bureau of Prisons has determined that release to home detention under this section will result in a substantial net reduction of costs to the Federal Government; and

children.label: vii
children.text: who has been determined by the Bureau of Prisons to be at no substantial risk of engaging in criminal conduct or of endangering any person or the public if released to home detention.

children.heading: Home detention
children.label: B Home detention
children.text: The term “home detention” has the same meaning given the term in the Federal Sentencing Guidelines as of , and includes detention in a nursing home or other residential long-term care facility.April 9, 2008

children.heading: Term of imprisonment
children.label: C Term of imprisonment
children.text: The term “term of imprisonment” includes multiple terms of imprisonment ordered to run consecutively or concurrently, which shall be treated as a single, aggregate term of imprisonment for purposes of this section.

children.label: i
children.text: is serving a term of imprisonment based on conviction for an offense or offenses that do not include any crime of violence (as defined in ), sex offense (as defined in ), offense described in , or offense under chapter 37 of title 18;section 16(a) of title 18section 20911(5) of this titlesection 2332b(g)(5)(B) of title 18

children.label: ii
children.text: satisfies the criteria specified in clauses (iii) through (vii) of subparagraph (A); and

children.label: I
children.text: in need of care at a nursing home, intermediate care facility, or assisted living facility, as those terms are defined in ; orsection 1715w of title 12

children.label: II
children.text: diagnosed with a terminal illness.

children.heading: Authorization for appropriations for Bureau of Prisons
children.label: h Authorization for appropriations for Bureau of Prisons
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to the Attorney General to carry out this section, $5,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2019 through 2023.
